steps.title: Identify the key clue about scholarship
steps.content:
The crucial phrase is “making it seem uniformly radical.” The word “seem” signals that scholars' emphasis on militant figures creates an inaccurate impression of the movement.

The passage then reveals (via Herrera) what scholars have overlooked: the diverse political orientations present in low-profile institutions.

steps.title: Match the function
steps.content:
The underlined portion describes a scholarly approach that creates a distorted picture by focusing only on militant figures.

The text confirms this distortion by showing what was actually obscured: the movement's ideological diversity.

The answer that names this function correctly identifies how a scholarly practice conceals important variation in the movement.

The wrong answers either claim scholars have changed their focus, introduce explanations not provided in the text, or identify a different kind of distortion entirely.

That matches Choice C — It describes a common approach to studying the Chicano movement that, according to the text, obscures the ideological diversity of the movement's participants..
